page: Amazon Web Services m5ad.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 256 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 2400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core tests, fall into the bottom 10%, reflecting the lower clock speed of the older AMD EPYC architecture. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. Cryptographic performance is a notable strength, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the top 25%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA-NI instruction sets.

Memory bandwidth and database operations, including Redis SET and static web serving, perform within the average range. The integrated 2400 GB NVMe SSD storage provides high-speed local scratch space, making this instance highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring high resource density and local storage without the overhead of network-attached storage. The instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, containerized microservices, and database workloads that can leverage the local NVMe storage, while it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or intensive LLM text generation.
description: The Amazon Web Services m5ad.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC 7571 processor and 256 GB of DDR4 memory. It includes 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 12 Gbps baseline network speed. While single-core benchmarks place the instance in the bottom 10% due to its 2.5 GHz clock speed, its multi-core and cryptographic capabilities perform at average to strong levels, with Passmark encryption scoring in the top 25%. The balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio combined with high-capacity local NVMe storage makes this instance a cost-efficient choice for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, and web serving that require fast local scratch space, though it is less suited for single-threaded or intensive machine learning inference tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5ad.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2400 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering stable multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform combining dedicated AMD EPYC processors with high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC 7571 processor and 256 GB DDR4 RAM
Includes 2400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
Delivers strong cryptographic performance, ranking in the top 25%
Features a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for multi-threaded databases, web serving, and containerized applications
